critic_raw:
DECAF uses a DAG-based causal GAN to remove bias from datasets. The causal DAG must be either specified manually or learned. If specified manually, the fairness guarantee depends entirely on whether the provided DAG is correct — if the true causal structure contains edges not in the assumed DAG (hidden confounders, missing causal paths), the debiasing procedure will leave causal bias pathways open. If the DAG is learned from observational data, the same limitation applies as in any observational causal discovery: the learned DAG is identifiable only up to a Markov equivalence class, and many alternative causal structures consistent with the observed data would produce different debiasing results.

The evaluation of synthetic dataset fairness requires choosing a downstream model and fairness metric pair, and downstream performance depends heavily on this choice. A synthetic dataset produced by DECAF that appears fair under demographic parity may be unfair under equalized odds or individual fairness, because these criteria are not equivalent and can be mutually incompatible. If the paper only evaluates one fairness metric, the 'debiased synthetic dataset' claim is metric-specific, not generally debiased.

GAN training instability is a practical problem for DECAF's replication: the quality of the synthetic data depends on whether the GAN converges, and GAN convergence is sensitive to hyperparameters, architecture choices, and initialization. The causal structure imposes additional constraints on the GAN training objective that can create additional instability modes. If the replication study finds results that differ from the original paper, attributing the discrepancy to the causal mechanism versus ordinary GAN variance is not possible without separate ablation of the causal DAG constraint.

The 'preprocessing' framing of DECAF assumes that fairness problems can be solved by transforming the data before model training. This is valid only if the bias is entirely in the data distribution and not in the model architecture, training procedure, or deployment context. If downstream models are capable of re-learning the removed biases from other correlated features in the synthetic data that DECAF did not identify as bias-carrying, the debiasing is illusory.
all_issues_raised: The causal DAG must be either manually specified or observationally learned — manual specification makes the fairness guarantee contingent on DAG correctness, while observational learning produces a DAG identifiable only up to Markov equivalence with multiple conflicting debiasing implications.

Fairness metrics (demographic parity, equalized odds, individual fairness) are mutually incompatible; evaluation under one metric does not imply fairness under others, and the 'debiased dataset' claim is metric-specific, not generally debiased.

GAN training instability is confounded with the causal DAG constraint during replication; performance discrepancies cannot be attributed to the causal mechanism versus ordinary GAN convergence variance without ablating the DAG constraint.
The preprocessing assumption fails if downstream models can re-learn removed biases from correlated features in the synthetic data that DECAF did not identify as bias-carrying.
all_issues_adjudicated: The causal DAG must be either manually specified or observationally learned — manual specification makes the fairness guarantee contingent on DAG correctness, while observational learning produces a DAG identifiable only up to Markov equivalence with multiple conflicting debiasing implications.

Fairness metrics (demographic parity, equalized odds, individual fairness) are mutually incompatible; evaluation under one metric does not imply fairness under others, and the 'debiased dataset' claim is metric-specific, not generally debiased.

GAN training instability is confounded with the causal DAG constraint during replication; performance discrepancies cannot be attributed to the causal mechanism versus ordinary GAN convergence variance without ablating the DAG constraint.
The preprocessing assumption fails if downstream models can re-learn removed biases from correlated features in the synthetic data that DECAF did not identify as bias-carrying.
